Title: NAC transcription factor GbNTL9 modifies the accumulation and organization of cellulose microfibrils to enhance cotton fiber strength

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL mapping and map-based cloning; expression analysis by RT-qPCR; transgenic overexpression with fiber-specific promoter (proGbEXPA2::NTL9BS41); RNA-seq; SEM/cytological observation of fiber cell wall and cellulose microfibrils
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of 20 DPA fibers from OE2 and Jin668, SRA: PRJNA1157997
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Fiber-specific overexpression of GbNTL9 increased fiber strength and promoted denser, more orderly cellulose microfibril accumulation without apparent penalty on fiber length or yield
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CESA4; CESA7; CESA8; COBL4; FLA11; FLA12; GUX1; GUX2; GUX3; IRX9; IRX12; IRX15; MYB46; MYB86; NAC043; NAC073
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAC transcription factor; NAM domain; C-terminal transmembrane domain
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in multiple tissues; strongly associated with fiber secondary wall thickening stage, increasing during 15–30 DPA and high in 20–30 DPA fibers
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides structural support and mechanical strength to cotton fiber cell walls, enhancing fiber strength for textile quality
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Secondary cell wall biogenesis and cellulose microfibril deposition/organization
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GbNTL9 carries a 24-bp insertion haplotype largely specific to G. barbadense; this haplotype is associated with stronger fiber and can be tracked by marker NTL9-24. Specific overexpression in fiber was beneficial, whereas constitutive overexpression of the Gb allele was reported unsuccessful due to severe developmental disruption
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field growth at Huazhong Agricultural University in summer 2023; fiber analyses focused on 20–30 DPA developmental stages
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value allele for marker-assisted selection; fiber-specific expression is recommended to improve fiber strength while minimizing tradeoffs; marker NTL9-24 can distinguish Gb and Gh haplotypes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation, predominantly negative regulation of secondary wall biosynthetic genes, delaying cellulose deposition and reorganizing microfibril assembly
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious yield or fiber-length penalty under fiber-specific overexpression; constitutive overexpression of GbNTL9 was unsuccessful and likely causes strong developmental disruption
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GbNTL9 was identified by fine mapping of major fiber-strength QTL qFS-A11-1. 2. Fiber-specific overexpression in upland cotton increased fiber strength by promoting dense and ordered cellulose microfibril arrangement. 3. GbNTL9 downregulated SCW-related genes including CESA4/7/8 and associated regulators, delaying cellulose deposition. 4. A 24-bp insertion haplotype from G. barbadense is associated with stronger fiber and enabled development of marker NTL9-24.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: A major positive regulator controlling fiber strength by altering cellulose microfibril deposition pattern rather than simply increasing wall thickness
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Ethylene signaling; upstream promoter-binding ERFs GhERF7; GhERF12; GhERF9; GhERF5
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher expression in BS41 than E22 during fiber secondary wall thickening, especially at 30 DPA
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with GhMYB6 as supported by Y2H and BiFC
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses or indirectly suppresses SCW-related genes, notably CESA4, CESA7, CESA8, FLA11, FLA12, NAC043 and MYB46

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based candidate identification; constitutive overexpression (35S::NTL9E22); CRISPR/Cas9 knockout of GhNTL9-A11; RT-qPCR; HVI fiber quality measurement; SEM and paraffin sections; Y2H/BiFC/Y1H
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq context from OE2 versus Jin668, SRA: PRJNA1157997
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Constitutive overexpression increased fiber strength but reduced fiber length and affected plant growth/yield; CRISPR knockout showed little effect on fiber strength, suggesting redundancy
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CESA4; CESA7; CESA8; FLA11; FLA12; NAC043; MYB46 and other SCW-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAC transcription factor; NAM domain; C-terminal transmembrane domain
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in all tissues; elevated during transition from fiber elongation to SCW thickening; active in 20–30 DPA fibers
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural cellulose organization underlying cotton fiber mechanical strength
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Secondary cell wall biosynthesis timing and cellulose microfibril organization
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Knockout had minimal phenotype on fiber strength, indicating possible functional redundancy. NTL9 acts downstream of ethylene signaling and interacts with GhMYB6
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cotton fiber developmental stage, especially 20–30 DPA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering fiber strength, but constitutive expression causes pleiotropic penalties; tissue-specific deployment is preferable
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation capability with downstream net repression of SCW gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Shorter fiber length, vigorous vegetative growth, reduced yield-related traits under constitutive overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhNTL9 was the candidate gene underlying qFS-A11-1. 2. 35S-driven overexpression increased fiber strength but reduced fiber length and yield-related traits. 3. GhNTL9 overexpression promoted denser cellulose microfibril arrangement while reducing expression of SCW biosynthetic genes. 4. GhNTL9 knockout had limited effect on fiber strength, suggesting redundancy.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator of fiber strength through remodeling secondary wall cellulose deposition timing and microfibril organization
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Ethylene-responsive TFs binding the promoter: GhERF7; GhERF12; GhERF9; GhERF5; also GhBHLH153; GhGATA15; GhCO3; GhBZIP44; GhGATA8
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed broadly in E22; elevated during fiber SCW thickening
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Physical interaction with GhMYB6 validated by BiFC; additional Y2H interactions with GhNFYC2, GhDPB, GhNFYC4 were identified but not BiFC-validated here
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Downregulates multiple SCW biosynthetic and regulatory genes, especially CESA4, CESA7, CESA8
